definitions.text: Reduced form of 'so that', used to express purpose; in order that.
examples: I got an earlier train to work so I'd have plenty of time to prepare for the meeting.
Eat your broccoli so you can have dessert.
It was so hot outside that all the plants died.
He was so good, they hired him on the spot.
I need a piece of cloth so long. [= this long]
I feel so much better now.
I so nearly lost my temper.

definitions.text: Agreeing with actual facts or reality; true.
examples: You are responsible for this, is that not so?

definitions.text: In that state or manner; with that attribute. A proadjective that replaces the aforementioned adjective phrase.
examples: "You're definitely not right about that." "I am so!" (→I am right about that).

definitions.text: Used after a pause for thought to introduce a new topic, question or story, or a new thought or question in continuation of an existing topic.
Used as a question to ask for further explanation of something said, often rhetorically or in a dismissive or impolite manner.
examples: "You park your car in front of my house every morning." — "So?"

definitions.text: Used as a meaningless filler word to begin a response to a question.
examples: What are you doing? / So I'm just fixing this shelf.
What time does the train leave? / So it leaves at 10 o'clock.

definitions.text: That which was previously mentioned; that.
examples: I'll become a loyal friend and remain so.
If that's what you really mean, then just say so.

definitions.text: A syllable used in solfège to represent the fifth note of a major scale. Sometimes syllable for both the second and the fifth.
Sometimes syllable for both the second and the fifth.
A type of dairy product, made especially in Japan between the seventh and tenth centuries, by reducing milk by boiling it.
